body.title: [triage] Hex's ollama serves METADATA but cannot INFER — /api/tags 200 in 2ms, /api/chat hangs forever for EVERY model — and HealthMonitor calls it healthy. All local inference is dead.
body.intention: I intend that a liveness probe tests the CAPABILITY THE SERVICE EXISTS FOR, not merely that the service answers the phone — because a probe that checks the wrong thing reports a dead service as healthy, and is indistinguishable from having no probe at all. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**MEASURED 2026-07-13** while closing T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal.

**⚠ THIS TICKET WAS FILED WITH A WRONG DIAGNOSIS AND IS CORRECTED IN PLACE. The correction is
kept visible, not silently overwritten (CP2: the wrong turn is data).** I first concluded "the
route points at a model that is not installed, and dispatch buries ollama's fast 404 under a 60s
timeout." **THAT WAS FALSE**, and it was false for an instructive reason: I probed **localhost**,
where `llama3.2:3b` is genuinely absent and genuinely 404s in 0.03s — but **the device never talks
to localhost.** `sources.py:1067` reads `INFERENCE_ENDPOINT`, which `igor.credentials.cfg` sets to
**`http://10.0.0.100:11434` (Hex)**. My repros ran without that env sourced; the device ran with it.
**I measured a different machine than the one under test, and the numbers were beautifully consistent
and entirely irrelevant.** The 60s timeout was never burying a 404 — there was no 404.

**THE TRUE DIAGNOSIS (all measured against Hex, 10.0.0.100):**
  * `GET /api/tags`  -> **200 in 0.002s**, lists 6 models: qwen3-coder:30b, deepseek-r1:32b,
    qwen2.5-coder:14b, deepseek-r1:14b, devstral-small-2:24b, **llama3.2:3b** (it IS installed).
  * `POST /api/chat` model=**llama3.2:3b** -> **HANGS. 65s, no response, no error.**
  * `POST /api/chat` model=**qwen2.5-coder:14b** -> **HANGS. 85s, no response, no error.**
  * Main-thread stack mid-hang (faulthandler): blocked in `socket.readinto` ->
    `http.client._read_status` <- `sources.py:388 in call` <- `device.py:709 in dispatch`. The TCP
    connection is ESTABLISHED and the request is SENT; Hex simply never sends a response line.
    (HealthMonitor's thread is parked in `threading.wait` — it is NOT the culprit.)

**SO: HEX'S OLLAMA SERVES METADATA BUT CANNOT INFER, FOR ANY MODEL.** It answers the phone
instantly and then says nothing, forever. This is not a slow model and not a missing model — the
inference path is wedged. **Every local-inference call in the rack — every `minion` and every
ollama-routed `worker` — burns 60 seconds and fails. The local tier is 100% dead right now.**

**THE DEFECT THAT IS OURS (the ops fault above is Hex's; this one is the code's):**
**HealthMonitor reports the source HEALTHY.** It probes REACHABILITY — the port answers, `/api/tags`
returns 200 — and reachability is exactly the property that is still TRUE while the service is
useless. **A probe that tests whether a service ANSWERS THE PHONE, when what you need to know is
whether it can DO THE JOB, reports a corpse as healthy.** It is a check indistinguishable from an
absent one — the same shape as the intent extractor's crash-wearing-CP1's-clothes, and as the
ossification hazard generally. The one live consumer that noticed (the intent extractor, freshly
fixed) reported `error_detail='inference: timed out'` — **which was TRUE, and was the only honest
signal anywhere in the stack.**

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/health_monitor.py (probes reachability, not
capability), sources.py:297-390 (`OllamaSource.call` — no fast-fail when a source accepts and never
answers), device.py:709 (dispatch), and NOTE `device.py:830-855` defines a SECOND `_ollama_chat`
that is not the live path (sources.py is) — a duplicate implementation worth chasing separately.

**Scope boundary:** IN — (a) HealthMonitor must probe the CAPABILITY (a tiny real completion, e.g.
`num_predict:1`, with a short timeout) and mark a source DOWN when it cannot infer, however cheerfully
it answers /api/tags; (b) a source that accepts a connection and never responds must fail FAST and
say WHAT it did (`no response from <endpoint> after Ns`), not surface a bare `socket.timeout('timed
out')`; (c) get Hex's ollama serving again (ops — restart; likely wedged/OOM on the Mac Studio).
OUT — model selection, and the extractor's record (done).

**Test plan / discriminating fixture:** a fake source that (1) answers `/api/tags` 200 but hangs on
`/api/chat` must be marked **DOWN**, while (2) a source that answers both is **UP**. A probe that
only checks /api/tags passes BOTH and fails this fixture — which is the entire point. Plus: dispatch
against (1) must return a named 'no response' error in well under the request timeout.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. A source that answers metadata but cannot complete is reported DOWN (fixture above).
  2. A hang surfaces a NAMED error identifying the endpoint and what it did, not a bare 'timed out'.
  3. Hex's ollama serves `/api/chat` again — VERIFIED by a real completion, not by /api/tags.
  4. The blast radius is stated as a number: how many rack calls hit the dead tier and for how long.

**BLOCKS:** the live success-rate re-measurement of the intent extractor (criterion 3 of
T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal) — no ollama-routed call can succeed until Hex infers.

**THE LESSON, WHICH IS THE SAME LESSON AS THE TICKET IT CAME FROM:** I got a fast, clean, confident
answer from the wrong machine and nearly shipped it as the diagnosis. The evidence was internally
consistent and completely wrong. **What saved it was capturing the stack from the PROCESS UNDER TEST
instead of reasoning about a reproduction I had built myself** (instrument-don't-probe). A repro that
does not run in the failing configuration is not a repro; it is a second experiment that happens to
be nearby.
